NKJV.1: This is a faithful saying: If a man desires the position of a bishop, he desires a good work.
NKJV.2: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, temperate, sober-minded, of good behavior, hospitable, able to teach;
NKJV.3: not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;
NKJV.4: one who rules his own house well, having his children in submission with all reverence
NKJV.5: (for if a man does not know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of the church of God?);
NKJV.6: not a novice, lest being puffed up with pride he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
NKJV.7: Moreover he must have a good testimony among those who are outside, lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
NKJV.8: Likewise deacons must be reverent, not double-tongued, not given to much wine, not greedy for money,
NKJV.9: holding the mystery of the faith with a pure conscience.
NKJV.10: But let these also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons, being found blameless.
NKJV.11: Likewise, their wives must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.
NKJV.12: Let deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
NKJV.13: For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a good standing and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
NKJV.14: These things I write to you, though I hope to come to you shortly;
NKJV.15: but if I am delayed, I write so that you may know how you ought to conduct yourself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
NKJV.16: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness:  God was manifested in the flesh,  Justified in the Spirit,  Seen by angels,  Preached among the Gentiles,  Believed on in the world,  Received up in glory.

ESV.1: The saying is trustworthy: If anyone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a noble task.
ESV.2: Therefore an overseer  must be above reproach, the husband of one wife,  sober-minded, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach,
ESV.3: not a drunkard, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money.
ESV.4: He must manage his own household well, with all dignity keeping his children submissive,
ESV.5: for if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he care for God's church?
ESV.6: He must not be a recent convert, or he may become puffed up with conceit and fall into the condemnation of the devil.
ESV.7: Moreover, he must be well thought of by outsiders, so that he may not fall into disgrace, into a snare of the devil.
ESV.8: Deacons likewise must be dignified, not double-tongued,  not addicted to much wine, not greedy for dishonest gain.
ESV.9: They must hold the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
ESV.10: And let them also be tested first; then let them serve as deacons if they prove themselves blameless.
ESV.11: Their wives likewise  must be dignified, not slanderers, but sober-minded, faithful in all things.
ESV.12: Let deacons each be the husband of one wife, managing their children and their own households well.
ESV.13: For those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and also great confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
ESV.14: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these things to you so that,
ESV.15: if I delay, you may know how one ought to behave in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, a pillar and buttress of the truth.
ESV.16: Great indeed, we confess, is the mystery of godliness: He  was manifested in the flesh, vindicated  by the Spirit,  seen by angels, proclaimed among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.

NASB.1: It is a trustworthy statement: if any man aspires to the office of overseer, it is a fine work he desires [to do].
NASB.2: An overseer, then, must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, prudent, respectable, hospitable, able to teach,
NASB.3: not addicted to wine or pugnacious, but gentle, peaceable, free from the love of money.
NASB.4: [He must be] one who manages his own household well, keeping his children under control with all dignity
NASB.5: (but if a man does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of the church of God?),
NASB.6: [and] not a new convert, so that he will not become conceited and fall into the condemnation incurred by the devil.
NASB.7: And he must have a good reputation with those outside [the church], so that he will not fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
NASB.8: Deacons likewise [must be] men of dignity, not double-tongued, or addicted to much wine or fond of sordid gain,
NASB.9: [but] holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
NASB.10: These men must also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons if they are beyond reproach.
NASB.11: Women [must] likewise [be] dignified, not malicious gossips, but temperate, faithful in all things.
NASB.12: Deacons must be husbands of [only] one wife, [and] good managers of [their] children and their own households.
NASB.13: For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a high standing and great confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
NASB.14: I am writing these things to you, hoping to come to you before long;
NASB.15: but in case I am delayed, [I write] so that you will know how one ought to conduct himself in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and support of the truth.
NASB.16: By common confession, great is the mystery of godliness: He who was revealed in the flesh, Was vindicated in the Spirit, Seen by angels, Proclaimed among the nations, Believed on in the world, Taken up in glory.

NLT.1: This is a trustworthy saying: “If someone aspires to be a church leader, he desires an honorable position.”
NLT.2: So a church leader must be a man whose life is above reproach. He must be faithful to his wife. He must exercise self-control, live wisely, and have a good reputation. He must enjoy having guests in his home, and he must be able to teach.
NLT.3: He must not be a heavy drinker or be violent. He must be gentle, not quarrelsome, and not love money.
NLT.4: He must manage his own family well, having children who respect and obey him.
NLT.5: For if a man cannot manage his own household, how can he take care of God’s church?
NLT.6: A church leader must not be a new believer, because he might become proud, and the devil would cause him to fall.
NLT.7: Also, people outside the church must speak well of him so that he will not be disgraced and fall into the devil’s trap.
NLT.8: In the same way, deacons must be well respected and have integrity. They must not be heavy drinkers or dishonest with money.
NLT.9: They must be committed to the mystery of the faith now revealed and must live with a clear conscience.
NLT.10: Before they are appointed as deacons, let them be closely examined. If they pass the test, then let them serve as deacons.
NLT.11: In the same way, their wives must be respected and must not slander others. They must exercise self-control and be faithful in everything they do.
NLT.12: A deacon must be faithful to his wife, and he must manage his children and household well.
NLT.13: Those who do well as deacons will be rewarded with respect from others and will have increased confidence in their faith in Christ Jesus.
NLT.14: I am writing these things to you now, even though I hope to be with you soon,
NLT.15: so that if I am delayed, you will know how people must conduct themselves in the household of God. This is the church of the living God, which is the pillar and foundation of the truth.
NLT.16:
Without question, this is the great mystery of our faith : Christ was revealed in a human body
and vindicated by the Spirit.
He was seen by angels
and announced to the nations.
He was believed in throughout the world
and taken to heaven in glory.

WEB.1: This is a faithful saying: if a man seeks the office of an overseer,  he desires a good work.
WEB.2: The overseer therefore must be without reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, sensible, modest, hospitable, good at teaching;
WEB.3: not a drinker, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;
WEB.4: one who rules his own house well, having children in subjection with all reverence;
WEB.5: (but if a man doesn’t know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of the assembly of God?)
WEB.6: not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
WEB.7: Moreover he must have good testimony from those who are outside, to avoid falling into reproach and the snare of the devil.
WEB.8: Servants,  in the same way, must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for money;
WEB.9: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
WEB.10: Let them also first be tested; then let them serve  if they are blameless.
WEB.11: Their wives in the same way must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.
WEB.12: Let servants  be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.
WEB.13: For those who have served well  gain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
WEB.14: These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly;
WEB.15: but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
WEB.16: Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God  was revealed in the flesh, justified in the spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, and received up in glory.

CSB17.1: This saying is trustworthy: <sup>ⓘ</sup> “If anyone aspires to be an overseer, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> he desires a noble work.”
CSB17.2: An overseer, <sup>ⓚ</sup> therefore, must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, self-controlled, sensible, <sup>ⓛ</sup> respectable, hospitable, <sup>ⓜ</sup> able to teach,
CSB17.3: not an excessive drinker, not a bully but gentle, not quarrelsome, not greedy. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.4: He must manage his own household competently and have his children under control with all dignity.
CSB17.5: (If anyone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of God’s church?)
CSB17.6: He must not be a new convert, or he might become conceited and incur the same condemnation as the devil. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.7: Furthermore, he must have a good reputation among outsiders, so that he does not fall into disgrace and the devil’s <sup>ⓑ</sup> trap.
CSB17.8: Deacons, <sup>ⓒ</sup> likewise, should be worthy of respect, not hypocritical, not drinking a lot of wine, not greedy for money,
CSB17.9: holding the mystery of the faith <sup>ⓓ</sup> with a clear conscience. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.10: They must also be tested first; if they prove blameless, then they can serve as deacons.
CSB17.11: Wives, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> too, must be worthy of respect, not slanderers, self-controlled, <sup>ⓖ</sup> faithful in everything.
CSB17.12: Deacons are to be husbands of one wife, managing their children and their own households competently.
CSB17.13: For those who have served well as deacons acquire a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
CSB17.14: I write these things to you, hoping to come <sup>ⓗ</sup> to you soon.
CSB17.15: But if I should be delayed, I have written so that you will know how people ought to conduct themselves in God’s household, <sup>ⓘ</sup> which is the church of the living God, <sup>ⓙ</sup> the pillar and foundation of the truth. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.16:
And most certainly, the mystery of godliness ⓛ is great: He [3] was manifested in the flesh, ⓜ
vindicated in the Spirit, ⓝ
seen by angels,
preached among the nations,
believed ⓐ on in the world,
taken up in glory. ⓑ

AMP.1: This is a faithful and trustworthy saying: if any man [eagerly] seeks the office of overseer (bishop, superintendent), he desires an excellent task.
AMP.2: Now an overseer must be blameless and beyond reproach, the husband of one wife, self-controlled, sensible, respectable, hospitable, able to teach,
AMP.3: not addicted to wine, not a bully nor quick-tempered and hot-headed, but gentle and considerate, free from the love of money [not greedy for wealth and its inherent power—financially ethical].
AMP.4: He must manage his own household well, keeping his children under control with all dignity [keeping them respectful and well-behaved]
AMP.5: (for if a man does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of the church of God?).
AMP.6: and He must not be a new convert, so that he will not [behave stupidly and] become conceited [by appointment to this high office] and fall into the [same] condemnation incurred by the devil [for his arrogance and pride].
AMP.7: And he must have a good reputation and be well thought of by those outside the church, so that he will not be discredited and fall into the devil's trap.
AMP.8: Deacons likewise must be men worthy of respect [honorable, financially ethical, of good character], not double-tongued [speakers of half-truths], not addicted to wine, not greedy for dishonest gain,
AMP.9: but upholding and fully understanding the mystery [that is, the true doctrine] of the [Christian] faith with a clear conscience [resulting from behavior consistent with spiritual maturity].
AMP.10: These men must first be tested; then if they are found to be blameless and beyond reproach [in their Christian lives], let them serve as deacons.
AMP.11: Women must likewise be worthy of respect, not malicious gossips, but self-controlled, [thoroughly] trustworthy in all things.
AMP.12: Deacons must be husbands of only one wife, and good managers of their children and their own households.
AMP.13: For those who have served well as deacons gain a high standing [having a good reputation among the congregation], and great confidence in the faith which is [founded on and centered] in Christ Jesus.
AMP.14: I hope to come to you before long, but I am writing these instructions to you
AMP.15: in case I am delayed, so that you will know how people ought to conduct themselves in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
AMP.16:
And great, we confess, is the mystery [the hidden truth] of godliness:
He (Jesus Christ) who was revealed in human flesh,
Was justified and vindicated in the Spirit,
Seen by angels,
Preached among the nations,
Believed on in the world,
Taken up in glory.

MSG.1: If anyone wants to provide leadership in the church, good!
MSG.2: But there are preconditions: A leader must be well-thought-of, committed to his wife, cool and collected, accessible, and hospitable. He must know what he's talking about,
MSG.3: not be overfond of wine, not pushy but gentle, not thin-skinned, not money-hungry.
MSG.4: He must handle his own affairs well, attentive to his own children and having their respect.
MSG.5: For if someone is unable to handle his own affairs, how can he take care of God's church?
MSG.6: He must not be a new believer, lest the position go to his head and the Devil trip him up.
MSG.7: Outsiders must think well of him, or else the Devil will figure out a way to lure him into his trap.
MSG.8: The same goes for those who want to be servants in the church: serious, not deceitful, not too free with the bottle, not in it for what they can get out of it.
MSG.9: They must be reverent before the mystery of the faith, not using their position to try to run things.
MSG.10: Let them prove themselves first. If they show they can do it, take them on.
MSG.11: No exceptions are to be made for women—same qualifications: serious, dependable, not sharp-tongued, not overfond of wine.
MSG.12: Servants in the church are to be committed to their spouses, attentive to their own children, and diligent in looking after their own affairs.
MSG.13: Those who do this servant work will come to be highly respected, a real credit to this Jesus-faith.
MSG.14: I hope to visit you soon, but just in case I'm delayed, I'm writing this letter so
MSG.15: you'll know how things ought to go in God's household, this God-alive church, bastion of truth.
MSG.16: This Christian life is a great mystery, far exceeding our understanding, but some things are clear enough: He appeared in a human body, was proved right by the invisible Spirit, was seen by angels. He was proclaimed among all kinds of peoples, believed in all over the world, taken up into heavenly glory.

NIV.1:
Overseers and Deacons
Here is a trustworthy saying: If anyone sets his heart on being an overseer, he desires a noble task.
NIV.2: Now the overseer must be above reproach, the husband of but one wife, temperate, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach,
NIV.3: not given to drunkenness, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money.
NIV.4: He must manage his own family well and see that his children obey him with proper respect.
NIV.5: (If anyone does not know how to manage his own family, how can he take care of God’s church?)
NIV.6: He must not be a recent convert, or he may become conceited and fall under the same judgment as the devil.
NIV.7: He must also have a good reputation with outsiders, so that he will not fall into disgrace and into the devil’s trap.
NIV.8: Deacons, likewise, are to be men worthy of respect, sincere, not indulging in much wine, and not pursuing dishonest gain.
NIV.9: They must keep hold of the deep truths of the faith with a clear conscience.
NIV.10: They must first be tested; and then if there is nothing against them, let them serve as deacons.
NIV.11: In the same way, their wives are to be women worthy of respect, not malicious talkers but temperate and trustworthy in everything.
NIV.12: A deacon must be the husband of but one wife and must manage his children and his household well.
NIV.13: Those who have served well gain an excellent standing and great assurance in their faith in Christ Jesus.
NIV.14: Although I hope to come to you soon, I am writing you these instructions so that,
NIV.15: if I am delayed, you will know how people ought to conduct themselves in God’s household, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
NIV.16:
Beyond all question, the mystery of godliness is great:
He appeared in a body,
was vindicated by the Spirit,
was seen by angels,
was preached among the nations,
was believed on in the world,
was taken up in glory.

NRSVCE.1: The saying is sure: whoever aspires to the office of bishop desires a noble task.
NRSVCE.2: Now a bishop must be above reproach, married only once, temperate, sensible, respectable, hospitable, an apt teacher,
NRSVCE.3: not a drunkard, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, and not a lover of money.
NRSVCE.4: He must manage his own household well, keeping his children submissive and respectful in every way —
NRSVCE.5: for if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how can he take care of God’s church?
NRSVCE.6: He must not be a recent convert, or he may be puffed up with conceit and fall into the condemnation of the devil.
NRSVCE.7: Moreover, he must be well thought of by outsiders, so that he may not fall into disgrace and the snare of the devil.
NRSVCE.8: Deacons likewise must be serious, not double-tongued, not indulging in much wine, not greedy for money;
NRSVCE.9: they must hold fast to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
NRSVCE.10: And let them first be tested; then, if they prove themselves blameless, let them serve as deacons.
NRSVCE.11: Women likewise must be serious, not slanderers, but temperate, faithful in all things.
NRSVCE.12: Let deacons be married only once, and let them manage their children and their households well;
NRSVCE.13: for those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
NRSVCE.14: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these instructions to you so that,
NRSVCE.15: if I am delayed, you may know how one ought to behave in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and bulwark of the truth.
NRSVCE.16:
Without any doubt, the mystery of our religion is great: He was revealed in flesh,
vindicated in spirit,
seen by angels,
proclaimed among Gentiles,
believed in throughout the world,
taken up in glory.

NET.1: This saying is trustworthy: “If someone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a good work.”
NET.2: The overseer then must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, an able teacher,
NET.3: not a drunkard, not violent, but gentle, not contentious, free from the love of money.
NET.4: He must manage his own household well and keep his children in control without losing his dignity.
NET.5: But if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he care for the church of God?
NET.6: He must not be a recent convert or he may become arrogant and fall into the punishment that the devil will exact.
NET.7: And he must be well thought of by those outside the faith, so that he may not fall into disgrace and be caught by the devil’s trap.
NET.8: Deacons likewise must be dignified, not two-faced, not given to excessive drinking, not greedy for gain,
NET.9: holding to the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
NET.10: And these also must be tested first and then let them serve as deacons if they are found blameless.
NET.11: Likewise also their wives must be dignified, not slanderous, temperate, faithful in every respect.
NET.12: Deacons must be husbands of one wife and good managers of their children and their own households.
NET.13: For those who have served well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
NET.14: I hope to come to you soon, but I am writing these instructions to you
NET.15: in case I am delayed, to let you know how people ought to conduct themselves in the household of God, because it is the church of the living God, the support and bulwark of the truth.
NET.16: And we all agree, our religion contains amazing revelation: He was revealed in the flesh, vindicated by the Spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among Gentiles, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.
